name: Community Development Corporation Of Long Island
description_short: Regional nonprofit CDFI providing affordable housing solutions, financial coaching, and community development services to Long Island residents since 1969.
description_long:
Community Development Long Island (CDLI) is a regional nonprofit organization founded in 1969 by government, business, and civic leaders to address Long Island's affordable housing crisis. The organization has evolved into a full-service collection of community development entities, including CDLI Parent Corporation, CDCLI Funding Corporation (a Certified Community Development Financial Institution), and CDCLI Housing Development Corporation. With over 115 community-centered professionals, CDLI serves more than 25,000 individuals annually across Nassau and Suffolk counties.

CDLI offers a comprehensive suite of programs addressing housing and economic stability. Core offerings include the Housing Choice Voucher Program (currently paused for new vouchers as of February 2024), homeownership preparation through their "Dreamers of Homeownership" program, financial coaching and resources, homeowner improvement and maintenance assistance, and Community Development Financial Institution lending options. They also facilitate rental opportunities and work with property developers to expand affordable housing stock throughout Long Island.

CDLI is distinguished by its status as the only NeighborWorks Organization on Long Island and its chartered membership in NeighborWorks America, a nationally connected network. The organization demonstrates exceptional fiscal integrity and service performance standards. Their integrated approach combines housing development with financial empowerment education, addressing both immediate housing needs and long-term economic stability. They explicitly focus on serving Long Island's most marginalized and compromised residents with fair housing goals and community integration priorities.

While CDLI provides valuable nonprofit services and financial coaching, prospective users should note that their Housing Choice Voucher Program is currently paused for new applications (as of February 2024 with no stated end date). The organization's services are geographically limited to Long Island (Nassau and Suffolk counties). Their lending products through the CDFI require qualification, and specific loan terms, rates, and APRs are not detailed on their public website.
pros: Certified Community Development Financial Institution (CDFI) offering lending options under 36% APR aligned with payday alternative standards
Only NeighborWorks Organization on Long Island with national accreditation and connection
Comprehensive financial coaching and resources program beyond just lending
Serves 25,000+ individuals annually with over 50 years of established operations since 1969
Full-service approach combining housing assistance, financial empowerment, and community development
Explicitly focuses on underserved populations and fair housing integration goals
High fiscal integrity and service performance standards documented
cons: Housing Choice Voucher Program suspended for new applicants as of February 2024 with no specified end date
Geographic service area limited to Long Island (Nassau and Suffolk counties only)
Specific loan terms, interest rates, and APRs not disclosed on public website
Qualification requirements for lending products not detailed; not all programs accessible to all residents
best_for: Long Island residents seeking affordable housing solutions and homeownership education
Low-to-moderate income families needing financial coaching and economic empowerment resources
Individuals preparing for homeownership with limited credit history or financial literacy
diagnosis: CDLI is best for Long Island residents seeking affordable housing and financial empowerment support from an established, accredited nonprofit CDFI. The main caveat is that their Housing Choice Voucher Program is currently suspended for new applicants with no stated timeline for reopening, and services are limited to Nassau and Suffolk counties only.
services: Housing Choice Voucher Program (currently paused for new applicants)
Financial coaching and financial fitness resources
Homeowner improvement and maintenance assistance programs
Community Development Financial Institution lending options
Rental opportunity matching and landlord partnership services
Fair housing counseling and integration support
Property developer partnership and affordable housing development
Community partner collaboration for sustainable housing solutions
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
